$comment: Self-derived finetype drift contract for crosswalk.edgar_gleif, checked by the in-engine finetype_validate@1 gate (the model-free `ft_validate` DuckDB table macro). ft_validate MELTS every column to VARCHAR, so this contract uses ONLY the string-surviving JSON-Schema keywords. ENFORCED: `enum` closed domains on key_type / tier / method / category / reg_status; `pattern` shapes on lei (ISO 17442: 18 alnum + 2 check digits), key (CIK digits or S…/CEF… series ids), as_of (ISO date), confidence (a [0,1] decimal string), jurisdiction, ticker; and `minLength`/`maxLength` envelopes. NOT ENFORCED, by design: numeric ranges — a `type: number`/`minimum`/`maximum` BACKFIRES once a column is VARCHAR-melted (it rejects 100% of rows), so confidence/key carry a string `pattern`, not a numeric range; NULLs — ft_validate treats NULL and empty/whitespace as INVISIBLE (no not-null enforcement), so nullable columns (reg_status, jurisdiction, ticker, company_name, legal_name) still pass; and object-level `required` (documentation only — the macro is per-column). Free-text fields (company_name, legal_name, corpus) carry LOOSE length envelopes (gross-corruption tripwires with headroom), not tight formats. Derived from build/edgar_gleif.parquet: every current row passes (a pass-through today), and a value outside a closed enum or a shape break trips it (a fail-closed drift tripwire for future Runs). Regenerate from the built Parquet with the same keyword discipline after an intentional schema change.
title: edgar_gleif
